verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is primarily an ER-resident type I transmembrane protein, but documented evidence shows a stable fraction localizes to the plasma membrane in some cell types. This PM-localized isoform (STIM1PM) has its own transmembrane domain and is stabilized at the surface via N-glycosylation, forming both type I and type II topologies. The protein reaches the outer face via its intrinsic TM anchor and maintains stable surface presence with functional roles in calcium signaling. This qualifies as dual_localization with documented cell-type-dependent or condition-dependent surface expression via the protein's own mechanism.
